input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
       Title="MyMauiApp"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard – single page, shown as its own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings – separate flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to show them how to set up a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml with a specific structure. This is a code generation/explanation request - I don't need to explore a codebase. Let me create the files directly.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout menu with:
   - "Dashboard" section (single page)
   - "Products" section with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
   - "Settings" page accessible from flyout
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage (navigated to programmatically)

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
       Title="MyMauiApp"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard – single page, shown as its own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings – separate flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the setup:

**AppShell.xaml** — Three `FlyoutItem`s define the flyout menu:

| Flyout Entry | Structure |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single `ShellContent` → `DashboardPage` |
| **Products** | Two `<Tab>` children → `ActiveProductsPage` & `ArchivedProductsPage` |
| **Settings** | Single `ShellContent` → `SettingsPage` |

**AppShell.xaml.cs** — `Routing.RegisterRoute` registers `ProductDetailsPage` so you can navigate to it programmatically:

```csharp
// From any products list page:
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

**Key points:**
- `ContentTemplate` + `DataTemplate` gives you **lazy loading** — pages are created only when first visited.
- `ProductDetailsPage` isn't in the XAML hierarchy, so it's registered as a code route and pushed onto the navigation stack.
- To receive the `id` parameter, decorate `ProductDetailsPage` with `[QueryProperty("ProductId", "id")]` or use `IQueryAttributable`.
